description_long: This object is a whole garlic bulb with a rounded, slightly irregular shape and a tapered end. The surface is covered in a thin, papery skin that is mostly intact, displaying a pale off-white color with subtle beige and brownish patches. The base of the bulb features a visible root tuft, which is brown and fibrous, contrasting with the smoother upper surface. The cloves are tightly packed together, forming a bulbous mass with faint segmentation lines visible beneath the skin. The overall texture appears matte with a slight roughness, and the bulb is small enough to be easily held in one hand. The object is natural and organic, typical of garlic found in grocery stores or urban markets.
description: A whole garlic bulb with pale, papery skin, visible root tuft, and tightly packed cloves. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with subtle beige and brown patches.
description_view_0: The bulb is seen from a side angle, showing its rounded body and the start of the tapered end, with smooth, pale skin and faint segmentation.
description_view_1: This view highlights the elongated, tapered end of the bulb and the smooth, slightly curved surface with subtle color variations.
description_view_2: The base of the bulb is visible, featuring a prominent, brown root tuft and more pronounced segmentation lines between cloves.
description_view_3: A close-up of the root end, showing the fibrous, brown root tuft and the circular arrangement of the cloves around the base.
attribute_bulb_segmentation: The bulb is whole with faint but visible segmentation lines; individual cloves are not fully separated but their outlines are discernible.
attribute_clove_arrangement: The cloves are tightly packed in a circular, layered arrangement, radiating from the center and forming a symmetrical bulb.
attribute_root_tuft_visibility: visible, with a small, brown, fibrous tuft at the base
attribute_stem_remnant: present, with a short, tapered, pale extension at the top
